name: FPSO CAPEX Ranking
author: Industry Perspective
description: Initial data model for the Top 10 upcoming FPSO projects by CAPEX case study. Captures attributed ranking claims (source, currency, horizon), reference operators and countries, and project rows with rank, stated CAPEX, and stage so the LinkedIn trigger visual can be checked and extended against primary sources.
source: case-studies/FPSO-capex-ranking: Giacomo Prandelli LinkedIn post and Top10_FPSO_CAPEX_projects.jpeg

entities.name: country
entities.brief: Host country for an FPSO project
entities.description: Country in which an FPSO project is located. Used to analyse geographic concentration of CAPEX (e.g. South Atlantic vs other basins).

fields.name: country_id
fields.description: Stable country identifier (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 preferred).

fields.name: country_name
fields.description: Human-readable country name as used in the ranking visual and case study narrative.

fields.name: region
fields.description: Optional coarse region label for portfolio framing (e.g. South Atlantic, West Africa).

entities.name: operator
entities.brief: Operating company on an FPSO project
entities.description: Company named as operator on a ranked FPSO project. Supports operator-concentration views of the investment cycle.

fields.name: operator_id
fields.description: Stable operator identifier (slug).

fields.name: operator_name
fields.description: Operator display name as shown on the ranking visual.

entities.name: capex_ranking
entities.description: A published ranking claim for upcoming FPSO projects by stated capital value. Records provenance (source URL, attribution, confidence) and the claimed portfolio total so values remain traceable until verified against primary disclosures.
entities.source: LinkedIn post by Giacomo Prandelli and accompanying visual

fields.name: ranking_id
fields.description: Stable identifier for this ranking snapshot.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the ranking as published.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: URL of the trigger source (LinkedIn post or other publication).

fields.name: source_attribution
fields.description: How the ranking should be attributed in narrative (author and channel).

fields.name: horizon_year
fields.description: End year of the ranking window (e.g. 2030 for projects to 2030).

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Currency code for stated CAPEX values (ISO 4217).

fields.name: claimed_total_m
fields.description: Combined CAPEX total claimed for the ranked set, in millions of the ranking currency. Treat as an attributed claim until reconciled to project rows and primary sources.

fields.name: capex_scope
fields.description: What the published values include. Unknown means the source does not establish whether values cover only each FPSO or the wider field development.

fields.name: capex_scope_status
fields.description: Whether the cost basis has been established from supporting evidence or remains an open discussion question.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Confidence in the ranking as a dataset: trigger sources are unverified claims until checked.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Interpretation notes: CAPEX definition unknowns, currency basis caveats, and case-study framing.

fields.name: projects
fields.description: FPSO projects included in this ranking, nested under the ranking snapshot.

fields.name: discussions
fields.description: Attributed comments that question, qualify, or contextualize this ranking without being treated as project facts.

entities.name: contractor
entities.brief: Contractor associated with an FPSO project
entities.description: A company identified as a contractor rather than the project operator. The separate entity prevents discussion-sourced contractor roles from being mislabeled as operators.

fields.name: contractor_id
fields.description: Stable contractor identifier (slug).

fields.name: contractor_name
fields.description: Contractor display name as stated by the source.

entities.name: fpso_project
entities.description: One FPSO project entry in a CAPEX ranking: stated value, operator, stage, host country, and rank. CAPEX is an attributed claim from the ranking source until a cost definition and primary source are recorded.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Stable project identifier (slug).

fields.name: ranking_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the parent CAPEX ranking snapshot; nests under capex_ranking.projects.

fields.name: project_name
fields.description: Project name as shown on the ranking visual.

fields.name: rank
fields.description: Position in the ranking by stated CAPEX (1 = highest).

fields.name: claimed_capex_m
fields.description: Stated project CAPEX in millions of the parent ranking currency. Definition (FPSO contract vs full field development) is unknown unless capex_definition is set.

fields.name: stage
fields.description: Project stage label from the ranking visual.

fields.name: operator_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the named operator.

fields.name: country_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the host country.

fields.name: capex_definition
fields.description: Optional clarification of what claimed_capex_million covers (e.g. FPSO contract value, full field development). Leave unset when unknown.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Optional project-level notes for verification status or naming variants.

entities.name: source_comment
entities.brief: Attributed discussion about a ranking claim
entities.description: A comment on the ranking source. Comments are modeled separately from project observations because they may raise a question or interpretation rather than assert a verified project fact.

fields.name: comment_id
fields.description: Stable identifier for the attributed comment.

fields.name: ranking_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the ranking discussed by this comment.

fields.name: source_type
fields.description: Provenance class, kept distinct from the trigger post and visual.

fields.name: author_name
fields.description: Display name of the commenter.

fields.name: author_url
fields.description: Public profile URL supplied with the comment attribution.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: URL of the post on which the comment appeared.

fields.name: comment_text
fields.description: Comment text as supplied or publicly displayed.

fields.name: issue_type
fields.description: The data-quality or interpretation issue raised by the comment.

fields.name: resolution_status
fields.description: Whether the issue has been answered with suitable evidence.

fields.name: interpretation
fields.description: How the comment affects use of the ranking data without turning the question into a factual assertion.

fields.name: project_mentions
fields.description: Projects suggested or discussed in this comment. These are not ranking entries unless separately supported by the trigger visual.

entities.name: discussion_project_mention
entities.description: A project named in discussion of the CAPEX ranking. It records what the commenter proposed without assigning a rank or CAPEX value and keeps comment provenance distinct from the original visual.

fields.name: mention_id
fields.description: Stable identifier for this source-specific project mention.

fields.name: comment_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the comment in which the project was named.

fields.name: project_name
fields.description: Project name as written in the comment.

fields.name: operator_id
fields.description: Foreign key to the operator identified by the commenter.

fields.name: country_id
fields.description: Host country inferred from the geographic description in the comment.

fields.name: contractor_id
fields.description: Foreign key to a contractor explicitly identified by the commenter.

fields.name: offshore_area
fields.description: Sea, basin, or other offshore location stated in the comment.

fields.name: inclusion_status
fields.description: Whether the mention is merely proposed or has been reconciled against the ranking criteria.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Limits on the assertion; comment mentions do not establish CAPEX, schedule, or eligibility for the top-ten ranking.
